# Phase 10.0.15 Succession Stability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated succession-control systems, centralized transition-enforcement systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve peaceful transition, succession legitimacy, anti-fracture continuity, and stable governance through leadership and institutional change after cultural memory and identity survivability doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional succession-stability doctrine.
- Peaceful-transition continuity philosophy.
- Institutional anti-fracture architecture.
- Governance continuity during transition standards.
- Constitutional succession legitimacy framework.
- Civilization continuity through change philosophy.
- Institutional transition survivability doctrine.
- Anti-fragmentation governance framework.
- Governance succession coherence standards.
- Constitutional continuity under transition architecture.
- Phase 10.0.15 succession stability summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No succession-stability layer creates runtime authority.
- No transition review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No anti-fracture layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No succession framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Succession stability remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, anti-fracture, and anti-authoritarian.
- Transition continuity preserves stewardship, not ownership.
- Anti-fracture controls preserve dissent and legitimacy without centralized enforcement.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated succession-control systems, centralized transition-enforcement systems, or execution systems.
